groups.title: Local food shops
groups.desc:
Questions 1-6
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Where to go • Kite Place – near the $21712$ Fish market • cross the $21713$ and turn right • best to go before $21714$ pm, earlier than closing time Organic shop • called '$21715$' • below a restaurant in the large, grey building • look for the large $21716$ outside Supermarket • take a $21717$ minibus, number 289

questions.analysis: 题干问的是Kite Place附近的具体位置。听力原文中明确提到“Kite Place”是“the area by the harbour”，即靠近港口的区域。因此，答案是“harbour/harbor”。
题干中的定位词是“cross”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“go over”。原文中提到“you have to go over the bridge and then it's on the right”，明确指出需要穿过桥（bridge），因此答案是“bridge”。

questions.answer: 3.30 / three thirty /  ½  / half 3 / half three
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是“3.30/three thirty/ ½ /half 3/half three”，因为听力原文中明确提到“as long as you get there by 3.30, you should be fine”，并且题干要求填写一个比关闭时间（4点）更早的时间。通过定位词和信号词的帮助，学生可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并从中提取正确的时间表达。
答案是“Rose”因为在听力原文中明确提到“it's the name of a flower. I know, it's 'Rose'”。题干中的定位词是“called”，在听力中对应的信号词是“it's the name of”，这提示了接下来会给出一个具体的名字。学生需要注意这些词，以便准确捕捉到答案。
这道题的正确答案是“sign”，因为听力原文中提到“a big sign on the pavement”，与题干中的“large”对应。考生需要注意同义词的使用，并在听力过程中抓住关键词和信号词，才能准确定位并填写正确答案。
答案是“purple”，因为在听力原文中明确提到“It's purple and the number is 289”。通过定位词“minibus”和“number 289”，考生可以迅速找到相关信息，并根据信号词“purple”确定答案。

groups.desc:
Questions 7-10
Complete the table below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

questions.analysis: 听力原文明确提到“It's a type of seaweed. I just ask for a handful and you fry it in butter. It's delicious!”并且拼写了这个单词“samphire”。题干中的定位词“handful”和“seaweed”在原文中得到了对应的信号词“handful”和“type of seaweed”。
答案是“melon”，因为在对话中，Leon明确表示他更喜欢“melon”而不是“mango”。考生需要注意对话中的细节，并根据定位词和信号词快速找到答案。
第9题的正确答案是“coconut”，因为在听力原文中，提到“spices”时，紧接着就提到了“coconut”。考生需要抓住题干中的定位词“spices”，并在听力过程中关注这个词出现后的内容。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和信号词来找到正确答案。
在对话中明确提到“the best are the strawberry ones”，并且题干要求填写的是一种tart的种类。通过定位词“Bakery”和“tart”，以及信号词“tarts”和“strawberry”，可以准确找到答案。

transcript.en: PART 1
LEON: Hi Shannon - how are you settling into your new flat?
transcript.cn: 里昂：嗨，香农。新公寓住的还习惯吗？

transcript.en: SHANNON: Really well, thanks.
transcript.cn: 香农：很好，谢谢。

transcript.en: LEON: You look like you're going shopping.
transcript.cn: 里昂：你看起来像是要去逛街。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Yes, I am. My cousins are coming to stay for a couple of days, and I have to cook for them.
transcript.cn: 香农：是的。我的表亲要来住几天，我得给他们做饭。

transcript.en: LEON: Well, there are plenty of places to buy food in Kite Place - it's the area by the harbour.
transcript.cn: 里昂：嗯，风筝广场有很多卖食物的地方，就在港口边上。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Oh, OK, I'll find that on the map. Thanks.
transcript.cn: 香农：哦，好的。我会在地图上找到的，谢谢。

transcript.en: LEON: What sort of food do you need to get?
transcript.cn: 里昂：你需要什么样的食物？

transcript.en: SHANNON: Well, neither of them eats meat but they both like fish.
transcript.cn: 香农：他们都不吃肉，不过都喜欢吃鱼。

transcript.en: LEON: Well, there's a really good fish market there.
transcript.cn: 里昂：那里有个很不错的鱼市。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Oh great - where is it exactly?
transcript.cn: 香农：太好了，具体在哪里？

transcript.en: LEON: It's at the far end of Kite Place, so you have to go over the bridge and then it's on the right.
transcript.cn: 里昂：在风筝广场的尽头，你得过桥，然后它就在右手边。

transcript.en: SHANNON: OK - is it open all day?
transcript.cn: 香农：全天营业吗？

transcript.en: LEON: It doesn't close until four, but I'd recommend going earlier than that - it does run out of some things.
transcript.cn: 里昂：四点关门，但是我建议你早点去，有的东西会卖完。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Oh, I don't want that to happen.
transcript.cn: 香农：哦，我可不想那样。

transcript.en: LEON: As long as you get there by 3.30, you should be fine. It's only 11 now, so plenty of time.
transcript.cn: 里昂：只要你在三点半之前赶到，应该没问题。现在才11点，时间很充裕。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Right.
transcript.cn: 香农：好的。

transcript.en: LEON: Do you need to buy vegetables too?
transcript.cn: 里昂：你也需要买蔬菜吗？

transcript.en: SHANNON: I do, and I want to avoid all the plastic packaging in the supermarket!
transcript.cn: 香农：是的。而且我不想买超市里塑料包装的那种。

transcript.en: LEON: Well, there's a really nice organic shop there. Now what's it called . . . it's the name of a flower. I know, it's 'Rose'.
transcript.cn: 里昂：那里有家很不错的有机食品店。店名叫什么来着……是一种花的名字。我想起来了，叫“Rose”。

transcript.en: SHANNON: That's a nice name.
transcript.cn: 香农：这名字不错。

transcript.en: LEON: Yeah - it sells vegetables and quite a lot of other stuff.
transcript.cn: 里昂：是的，这家店卖蔬菜和很多其他的东西。

transcript.en: SHANNON: And where's that?
transcript.cn: 香农：在哪里呢？

transcript.en: LEON: Well, as you reach the market, you'll see a big grey building on your left - I think it used to be a warehouse. Anyway, now it's a restaurant upstairs, but the ground floor has two shops either side of the entrance and it's the one on the left.
transcript.cn: 里昂：嗯，当你到市场，你会看到左手边有一栋灰色的大房子--我想它以前是个仓库。总之，现在楼上是一家餐馆，底层入口两侧有两家店铺，左边那家就是。

transcript.en: SHANNON: That's easy enough.
transcript.cn: 香农：这就好办了。

transcript.en: LEON: You can't miss it - there's also a big sign on the pavement so you can look for that.
transcript.cn: 里昂：你不会找不到的——人行道上还有一个大标志，你可以找找看。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Fine! I guess if I need anything else, I'll have to go to the supermarket.
transcript.cn: 香农：好吧! 我想如果我还需要什么的话，我就得去超市了。

transcript.en: LEON: Yeah - you should be able to get everything you need, but there's a minibus that goes to the supermarket if you need it.
transcript.cn: 里昂：是的，你应该可以得到你需要的一切，但是如果你需要的话，有一辆小巴士可以去超市。

transcript.en: It's purple and the number is 289.
transcript.cn: 是紫色的车，289路。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Thanks, that's great.
transcript.cn: 香农：谢谢，太好了。

transcript.en: LEON: So what do you need to get at the fish market?
transcript.cn: 里昂：你去鱼市买点什么？

transcript.en: The salmon is always very good and the shellfish.
transcript.cn: 三文鱼和贝类都很不错。

transcript.en: SHANNON: I'm going to make a curry, I think, and I need about 12 prawns for that.
transcript.cn: 香农：我想做一道咖喱，需要十二只虾。

transcript.en: LEON: They'll have plenty of those.
transcript.cn: 里昂：他们那里有很多。

transcript.en: SHANNON: OK.
transcript.cn: 香农：好的。

transcript.en: LEON: Have you ever tried samphire?
transcript.cn: 里昂：你吃过海蓬子吗？

transcript.en: SHANNON: No - what's that?
transcript.cn: 香农：没有，那是什么？

transcript.en: LEON: It's a type of seaweed. I just ask for a handful and you fry it in butter. It's delicious!
transcript.cn: 里昂：是一种海藻。只要一把，然后用黄油煎一下，非常好吃！

transcript.en: SHANNON: Oh, I might try that - how do you spell it?
transcript.cn: 香农：我可以试试，怎么拼呢？

transcript.en: LEON: It's S-A-M-P-H-I-R-E.
transcript.cn: 里昂：是S-A-M-P-H-I-R-E。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Great - it's always good to try something different.
transcript.cn: 香农：很好，尝试不同的东西总是好的。

transcript.en: LEON: Yeah.
transcript.cn: 里昂：没错。

transcript.en: SHANNON: I'll see what beans they have in the organic shop and I think I'll get something for dessert there.
transcript.cn: 香农：我去看看有机商店里有什么豆子，我想我还会在那里买些甜点。

transcript.en: LEON: How about a mango?
transcript.cn: 里昂：芒果怎么样。

transcript.en: SHANNON: I'm not sure - they're not always ripe.
transcript.cn: 香农：不确定，芒果不一定熟透了。

transcript.en: I'd prefer a melon - it's bigger too.
transcript.cn: 我更喜欢甜瓜，也比较大。

transcript.en: LEON: Good idea. The owner also sells a lot of spices there that you can put in a curry, and things like coconut.
transcript.cn: 里昂：好主意。店主还卖很多香料，你可以放在咖喱里面，还有椰子一类的东西。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Oh, that's very helpful. I'll have a look.
transcript.cn: 香农：这很有用，我去看看。

transcript.en: LEON: No problem.
transcript.cn: 里昂：没问题。

transcript.en: SHANNON: I know bread doesn't really go with curry but I always like to have some in case.
transcript.cn: 香农：我知道面包和咖喱不搭，但我总是喜欢买一些备着。

transcript.en: LEON: As I said - all the bread is home-made and there's lots of variety. I like the brown bread myself.
transcript.cn: 里昂：就像我说的，所有的面包都是自制的，而且品种繁多。我自己喜欢黑面包。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Mm, sounds good.
transcript.cn: 香农：听起来不错。

transcript.en: LEON: They sell other things there too.
transcript.cn: 里昂：那里还卖其他东西。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Like cakes? I love chocolate cake.
transcript.cn: 香农：比如蛋糕吗？我喜欢巧克力蛋糕。

transcript.en: LEON: Well - not that, but they have a whole range of tarts and the best are the strawberry ones.
transcript.cn: 里昂：不是那些，但有一系列的蛋挞，最好吃的是草莓蛋挞。

transcript.en: SHANNON: Perfect - hopefully I won't even have to go to the supermarket!
transcript.cn: 香农：太好了，希望我不用去超市了。

groups.title: Information
groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
What information is given about each of the following festival workshops?
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 11–16.

list.title: B
list.content: will be led by a prize-winning author

list.title: C
list.content: is aimed at children with a disability

list.title: F
list.content: is aimed at a specific age group

list.title: H
list.content: raises awareness of a particular culture

questions.title: Superheroes
questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为听力原文明确提到“Superheroes”是为听力受损的儿童设计的活动。学生在听力过程中应特别注意定位词和信号词，以便快速准确地找到答案。

questions.title: Just do it
questions.analysis: 正确答案是D，因为“Just do it”涉及角色扮演（role play），这是一种戏剧活动（drama activity）。

questions.title: Count on me
questions.analysis: 正确答案是F（is aimed at a specific age group），因为听力原文明确提到“Count on me”是针对13-14岁孩子的活动，这表明它是针对特定年龄组的。

questions.title: Speak up
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是G，因为“Speak up”工作坊的主题是心理健康，特别是孤独感。通过抓住“mental health”和“loneliness”这两个信号词，可以确定答案是“G. explores an unhappy feeling”。

questions.title: Jump for joy
questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为听力原文明确提到“Jump for joy”是由获得金奖的作者Nina Karan领导的活动。学生在听力过程中应特别关注与奖项相关的词汇，并结合题干中的定位词“Jump for joy”来确定答案。

questions.title: Sticks and stones
questions.analysis: 正确答案是H，因为“Sticks and stones”这个活动的主要目的是帮助孩子们了解他们的牙买加文化根源，这与“raises awareness of a particular culture”完全吻合。

groups.title: Which TWO reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?
groups.desc:
Questions 17-18
Choose two letters from the A-E
Write your answers in boxes 17-18 on your answer sheet.

list.title: A
list.content: It will appeal to both boys and girls.

list.title: E
list.content: It deals with an important topic.

collect_option.title: Which TWO reasons does the speaker give for recommending Alive and Kicking?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 D 和 E。D 选项 "It is funny" 对应的原文是 "this one actually made me cry with laughter"，说明这本书很有趣。E 选项 "It deals with an important topic" 对应的原文是 "It deals with the serious issue of immigration"，说明这本书涉及一个重要的话题。其他选项在原文中没有提到或与原文内容不符。

这道题的正确答案是 D 和 E。D 选项 "It is funny" 对应的原文是 "this one actually made me cry with laughter"，说明这本书很有趣。E 选项 "It deals with an important topic" 对应的原文是 "It deals with the serious issue of immigration"，说明这本书涉及一个重要的话题。其他选项在原文中没有提到或与原文内容不符。

groups.title: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give to parents about reading?
groups.desc:
Questions 19-20
Choose TWO letters, A-E.
Write your answers in boxes 19-20 on your answer sheet.

list.title: A
list.content: Encourage children to write down new vocabulary.

list.title: B
list.content: Allow children to listen to audio books.

list.title: D
list.content: Give children a choice about what they read.

list.title: E
list.content: Only read aloud to children until they can read independently.

collect_option.title: Which TWO pieces of advice does the speaker give to parents about reading?

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 B 和 C。通过分析听力材料中的具体句子和关键词，可以确定推荐这本书的原因是它帮助孩子们学习新词汇（B）和书中的搞笑插图（C）。学生需要注意定位词和信号词，集中注意力在听到这些词时捕捉相关信息。
本题的正确答案是 B 和 C。通过分析听力材料中的具体句子和关键词，可以确定推荐这本书的原因是它帮助孩子们学习新词汇（B）和书中的搞笑插图（C）。学生需要注意定位词和信号词，集中注意力在听到这些词时捕捉相关信息。

transcript.en: PART 2
PRESENTER: The children's book festival is coming up again soon and here to tell us all about it is the festival's organiser, Jenny Morgan. So tell us what we can expect this year, Jenny.
transcript.cn: 主持人：儿童图书节马上又要开始了，在这里向我们介绍的是儿童图书节的组织者珍妮-摩根（Jenny Morgan）。珍妮，请告诉我们今年有哪些活动。

transcript.en: JENNY: Well, as usual we've got five days of action-packed exciting events for children, with writers coming from all over the country getting involved.
transcript.cn: 珍妮：和往年一样，我们将为孩子们准备五天精彩纷呈的活动，全国各地的作家都将参与其中。

transcript.en: Just to give you an idea of what's on offer in the workshops, first of all, there's a very special event called Superheroes.
transcript.cn: 为了让你们了解一下工作坊里都有些什么，首先，有一个非常特别的活动，叫做“超级英雄”。

transcript.en: This is a chance for deaf children to share their reading experiences with author Madeleine Gordon, who is herself hearing impaired.
transcript.cn: 这是一个让聋哑儿童与作家马德琳-戈登（Madeleine Gordon）分享阅读经验的机会，她本人也有听力障碍。

transcript.en: 'Just do it' is a practical workshop led by the well-known illustrator Mark Keane.
transcript.cn: “做就是了”是一个由著名插画家马克-基恩（Mark Keane）主持的实践活动。

transcript.en: He'll take participants on a magical journey to faraway lands with an opportunity for aspiring actors to do some role play.
transcript.cn: 他将带领参与者踏上远方的魔幻之旅，并为有抱负的演员提供角色扮演的机会。

transcript.en: 'Count on me' is an inspiring and entertaining look at the issues of friendship for 13-14-year-olds.
transcript.cn: “相信我”是一个针对 13-14 岁青少年的关于友谊问题的励志且有趣的活动。

transcript.en: It looks at some of the friendships described in popular books and asks participants to compare these with their own experiences.
transcript.cn: 它着眼于一些流行书籍中描述的友谊，并要求参与者将这些友谊与自己的经历进行比较。

transcript.en: 'Speak up' is part of a series of workshops on the subject of mental health.
transcript.cn: “大声说出来”是关于心理健康主题系列活动的一部分。

transcript.en: This is a creative writing workshop encouraging children to describe situations where young people experience loneliness.
transcript.cn: 这是一个创意写作活动，鼓励儿童描述青少年经历孤独的情景。

transcript.en: A recent survey revealed that children can be lonely even when they're at home with their families.
transcript.cn: 最近的一项调查显示，即使孩子们和家人在一起，他们也会感到孤独。

transcript.en: 'Jump for joy', as many of you will know, is the heart-warming, best-selling story by Nina Karan about a young girl's trip to visit her relatives in India.
transcript.cn: 你们很多人都知道，妮娜 · 卡兰(Nina Karan)的畅销小说《欢呼雀跃》讲述了一个小女孩去印度探亲的故事。

transcript.en: It recently received the gold medal at the Waterford Awards. Nina will get children to celebrate the word 'joy' by writing a poem.
transcript.cn: 该书最近获得了沃特福德奖的金奖。妮娜会让孩子们写一首诗来赞美 "快乐 "这个词。

transcript.en: 'Sticks and stones' is the beautifully illustrated picture book for young readers about a community who organise an African-Caribbean festival to help local children learn about their Jamaican roots.
transcript.cn: 《棍棒和石头》是一本为小读者创作的精美插图绘本，讲述了一个社区组织非洲-加勒比节，帮助当地儿童了解他们的牙买加根源。

transcript.en: This will be a musical event where children will have the chance to play steel drums.
transcript.cn: 这将是一次音乐盛会，孩子们将有机会演奏钢鼓。

transcript.en: This is bound to be very popular, so please book as soon as possible.
transcript.cn: 这个活动一定会很受欢迎，请尽快预订。

transcript.en: PRESENTER: Thanks Jenny. That all sounds really interesting.
transcript.cn: 主持人：谢谢珍妮。听起来很有意思。

transcript.en: I'm just wondering if you have a favourite book you could recommend for our readers?
transcript.cn: 我想知道你有没有最喜欢的一本书可以推荐给我们的读者？

transcript.en: JENNY: It's hard to choose, but Alive and Kicking is definitely worth mentioning.
transcript.cn: 珍妮：很难选择，但《活蹦乱跳》绝对值得一提。

transcript.en: You won't have heard of the writer as it's her first book - which is really impressive.
transcript.cn: 你可能没听说过这位作家，因为这是她的第一本书——这确实令人印象深刻。

transcript.en: It's basically the teenage diary of a boy from Somalia who comes to live in the UK.
transcript.cn: 这本书主要讲述了一个从索马里来到英国生活的男孩的青少年日记。

transcript.en: It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier, etc.
transcript.cn: 这本书讲述了很多移民问题，以及男孩在学校和语言障碍等方面必须面对的所有挑战。

transcript.en: Usually, books like this are quite sad, but this one actually made me cry with laughter.
transcript.cn: 一般来说，这样的书都比较悲伤，但这本书却让我笑中带泪。

transcript.en: On each page, there are simple but hilarious black and white stick drawings of the boy with his friends and teachers.
transcript.cn: 在每一页上，都有简单和搞笑的黑白条画，画的是这个男孩与他的朋友和老师。

transcript.en: At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children's vocabulary.
transcript.cn: 在每篇日记的末尾，都有小男孩每天学习的新英语单词，这可能有助于扩展一些孩子的词汇量。

transcript.en: PRESENTER: I think my kids would enjoy that.
transcript.cn: 主持人：我想我的孩子们会喜欢的。

transcript.en: What about any advice for parents on how to encourage their children to read more?
transcript.cn: 对于父母如何鼓励孩子多读书，有什么建议吗？

transcript.en: JENNY: Well, this is something I get asked about a lot.
transcript.cn: 珍妮：我经常被问到这个问题。

transcript.en: There are so many distractions for kids these days that it can be hard to find time for reading.
transcript.cn: 如今，现在有太多让孩子分心的东西，他们很难抽出时间来阅读。

transcript.en: One thing I'd say is to make time to sit down with your child and share books with them.
transcript.cn: 我想说的一件事是，抽出时间和孩子坐在一起，与他们分享书籍。

transcript.en: A lot of parents give up reading aloud to their children as soon as they learn to read independently, but this is a mistake.
transcript.cn: 很多家长在孩子学会独立阅读后就放弃了朗读，其实这是不合适的。

transcript.en: It's good to read more advanced books to them as it helps to develop their vocabulary.
transcript.cn: 为他们朗读更进阶的书籍很有好处，因为这有助于扩展他们的词汇量。

transcript.en: If you don't have time for this, then let them listen to audio books.
transcript.cn: 如果您没有时间，那就让他们听有声读物吧。

transcript.en: Often, they'll want to read books they've listened to for themselves.
transcript.cn: 通常，他们会想读一些自己听过的书。

transcript.en: I think it's a good idea to make a mental note of the type of books your child is reading - often they just read the same genre all the time, which can get a bit boring.
transcript.cn: 我认为，最好在脑中记下您孩子正在阅读的书籍类型--他们往往总是阅读同一类型的书籍，这可能会让他们感到有些乏味。

transcript.en: You can introduce new authors and genres to them. Librarians should be able to help you with this.
transcript.cn: 您可以向他们介绍新的作者和类型。图书管理员应该可以在这方面帮到你。

transcript.en: PRESENTER: Well Jenny, I think that's really useful. . . .
transcript.cn: 主讲人：珍妮，我觉得这很有用……

groups.title: Science experiment for Year 12 students
groups.desc:
Questions 21-25
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: How does Clare feel about the students in her Year 12 science class?

options.title: A
options.content: worried that they are not making progress

options.title: B
options.content: challenged by their poor behaviour in class

options.title: C
options.content: frustrated at their lack of interest in the subject

questions.analysis: Clare对她的Year 12科学班级学生感到沮丧，因为学生对科学缺乏兴趣和参与感。她提到学生没有纪律问题，测试结果也不错，但学生没有真正的参与感，这表明她对学生缺乏兴趣感到沮丧。因此，正确答案是C. frustrated at their lack of interest in the subject。

questions.title: How does Jake react to Clare’s suggestion about an experiment based on children’s diet?

options.title: A
options.content: He is concerned that the results might not be meaningful.

options.title: B
options.content: He feels some of the data might be difficult to obtain.

options.title: C
options.content: He suspects that the conclusions might be upsetting.

questions.analysis: Jake 对 Clare 的实验建议表示担忧，主要是因为获取儿童的医疗记录会涉及隐私问题，数据获取会很困难。因此，答案是 B. He feels some of the data might be difficult to obtain.

questions.title: What problem do they agree may be involved in an experiment involving animals?

options.title: A
options.content: Any results may not apply to humans.

options.title: B
options.content: It may be complicated to get permission.

options.title: C
options.content: Students may not be happy about animal experiments.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为对话中明确提到实验结果对人类不一定适用，而其他选项在对话中并没有被认为是主要问题。克莱尔和杰克讨论了实验结果的局限性，特别是实验结果是否适用于人类。

questions.title: What question do they decide the experiment should address?

options.title: A
options.content: Are mice capable of controlling their food intake?

options.title: B
options.content: Does an increase in sugar lead to health problems?

options.title: C
options.content: How much do supplements of different kinds affect health?

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A，因为听力材料中明确提到实验的重点是“whether mice can control their own diet”，即“老鼠是否能够控制自己的饮食”。通过抓住定位词和信号词，学生可以更准确地定位到相关信息，从而选择正确答案。

questions.title: Clare might also consider doing another experiment involving
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C，因为在听力材料中明确提到可以进行一个后续实验，涉及到让一些老鼠更活跃（跑轮子），而另一些老鼠则不怎么活动。这与选项C中的“varying amounts of exercise”直接对应。通过抓住定位词“another experiment”和信号词“follow-up experiment”，并结合听力内容中的关键词“active”和“running on a wheel”，可以准确选择答案C。

groups.desc:
Questions 26-30
Complete the flowchart below.
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–H, next to Questions 26–30.

questions.title: Choose mice which are all the same
questions.analysis: 题干要求选择相同的老鼠，定位词是“same”。在听力原文中，Jake提到“all young ones, not a mixture of old and young”，这句话明确指出了老鼠的年龄应该是相同的，即都是年轻的。因此，答案是“young”。如果学生基础较差，可以通过抓住关键词“same”并注意听到与之相关的描述来找到答案。

questions.title: Divide the mice into two groups, each with a different
questions.analysis: 在听力材料中，Clare 和 Jake 讨论了如何区分两组老鼠。Clare 提到可以给一组老鼠打标签（tag）或做标记（mark），而 Jake 提出了使用食用色素（food colouring）的建议。题干中提到“each with a different”，即每组老鼠有不同的标记方法。根据对话内容，最合适的答案是“food colouring”，因为这是明确提到的区分方法。

questions.title:
Put each group in a separate cage. 
 Feed group A commercial mouse food. 
 Feed group B the same, but also sugar contained in
questions.analysis: 在听力材料中，Clare 和 Jake 讨论了给实验组的老鼠添加糖的问题。Jake 问 Clare 是否直接给老鼠糖（straight sugar），Clare 回答说可能更好的是给它们一些含糖的谷物（cereal with it）。因此，正确答案是“cereal”，因为这是他们最终决定的包含糖的食物形式。通过定位词“sugar contained”，我们可以迅速找到相关的讨论部分，并通过信号词“straight sugar”确认答案所在的句子。理解“cereal”作为替代方案的提及是解答这道题的关键。

questions.title:
Take measurements using an electronic scale. 
 Place them in a weighing chamber to prevent
questions.analysis: 第29题的正确答案是“B”。题干中的定位词是“weighing chamber”，在听力原文中也出现了相同的词汇。答案句明确提到称重室的作用是“to stop the mice from running away”，即防止老鼠跑走。因此，答案是“running away”。

questions.title: Do all necessary
questions.analysis: 题目要求填入的内容是实验步骤的最后一步，涉及到所有必要的操作。听力原文中提到“measurements”后，紧接着提到“average”和“standard deviation”，这两个词是计算实验数据时的必要步骤。因此，答案是“standard deviation”。通过定位词“necessary”，我们可以在听力材料中找到相关的信号词“standard deviation”，从而确定答案。

transcript.en: PART 3
CLARE: Hi Jake. How are you getting on with the practical teaching?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：你好，杰克。你的实践教学进展如何？

transcript.en: JAKE: It's harder than I expected, but I've got some great classes. How about you?
transcript.cn: 杰克：比我想象的要难，但我上了几堂很棒的课。你呢？

transcript.en: CLARE: Not brilliant. I'm really struggling with my Year 12 science class.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：不怎么样。我12年级的科学课上的很吃力。

transcript.en: JAKE: Are they hard to control?
transcript.cn: 杰克：学生很难管教吗？

transcript.en: CLARE: Well, I don't have discipline problems as such.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：不是纪律的问题。

transcript.en: It's just that they don't seem to think that science has anything to do with their lives. It's depressing.
transcript.cn: 只是他们似乎不认为科学与他们的生活有任何关系，真让人沮丧。

transcript.en: They listen to what I say, and I gave them a test last week and the results weren't too bad, but there's no real engagement.
transcript.cn: 他们听我讲课，我上周给他们做了个测试，结果还不错，但他们没有真正参与进来。

transcript.en: JAKE: Right.
transcript.cn: 杰克：这样啊。

transcript.en: CLARE: And as part of my teaching practice, I have to design an experiment for them to do.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：作为教学实践的一部分，我必须为他们设计一个实验。

transcript.en: I was wondering about something on the children's diets . . . you know, asking them to record what they eat and maybe linking it to their state of health.
transcript.cn: 我在想关于孩子们饮食的问题......你知道的，让他们记录下他们吃了什么，也许还能和他们的健康状况联系起来。

transcript.en: JAKE: Mmm. Let's think. So your methodology would involve the children recording what they eat.
transcript.cn: 杰克：嗯，让我们想想。所以你的方法是让孩子们记录他们吃了什么吗？

transcript.en: OK, but you'd also need to have access to the children's medical records and I don't think people would be happy about that; confidentiality would be an issue.
transcript.cn: 好吧，但你还需要查阅孩子们的医疗记录，我想人们不会乐意这样做；保密性也是个问题。

transcript.en: If you could get the right data, the conclusions might be significant, but I suspect it's not going to be easy.
transcript.cn: 如果你能得到正确的数据，结论可能很有意义，但我怀疑这并不容易。

transcript.en: CLARE: Right.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：没错。

transcript.en: JAKE: Have you thought about doing an experiment using animals?
transcript.cn: 杰克：你有没有想过用动物做实验？

transcript.en: CLARE: Wouldn't that be upsetting for the children?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：那不会让孩子们难过吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: Well, the animals don't have to be harmed in any way.
transcript.cn: 杰克：嗯，动物不必收到任何伤害。

transcript.en: It could just be an experiment where they're given a certain diet and the effects are observed.
transcript.cn: 这可能只是一个给它们特定饮食并观察其效果的实验。

transcript.en: CLARE: Would I have to get permission to use animals?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：用动物进行实验需要得到许可吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: Yes, you'd have to submit an outline of the experiment and fill in a form, but it's quite straightforward.
transcript.cn: 杰克：是的，你得提交一份实验大纲，然后填一张表格，不过这很简单。

transcript.en: CLARE: But if we found out that, say, a particular diet affects the health of animals, the same thing wouldn't necessarily be true for people, would it?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：但是如果我们发现，比如说，某种特定的饮食会影响动物的健康，那么同样的事情不一定适用于人类，不是吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: No that's true, but the findings for any experiment are going to be limited. It's inevitable.
transcript.cn: 杰克：没错，但是任何实验的结果都是有限的。这是不可能避免的。

transcript.en: CLARE: I suppose so. So what animals could I use to investigate the effects of diet? Mice?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：我想也是。那么我可以用什么动物来研究饮食的影响呢？老鼠吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: Yes. You'd need experimental mice - ones that have been specially bred for experiments.
transcript.cn: 杰克：是的。你需要实验小鼠--专门为实验培育的小白鼠。

transcript.en: OK, so what will your experiment be investigating exactly?
transcript.cn: 好吧，那你的实验具体要研究什么？

transcript.en: CLARE: Well, something to do with nutrition.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：和营养相关。

transcript.en: So maybe we could look at food supplements . . . things like extra iron and extra protein, and their impact on health.
transcript.cn: 也许我们可以看看食物补充剂，比如额外的铁和蛋白质，以及它们对健康的影响。

transcript.en: JAKE: Mmm. That might be rather broad. Maybe just look at the effects of one supplement, like sugar, on the health of the mice?
transcript.cn: 杰克：嗯。这可能比较宽泛。也许只研究一种补充剂，比如糖，对老鼠健康的影响？

transcript.en: CLARE: In fact, maybe the focus could be on whether mice can control their own diet. . . .
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：事实上，也许重点可以放在小鼠能否控制自己的饮食上. . .

transcript.en: JAKE: So, what happens when they have access to more sugar, that they don't really need?
transcript.cn: 杰克：那么，当他们有了更多并不需要的糖，将会发生什么？

transcript.en: CLARE: Exactly. Do they eat it or do they decide to leave it?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：没错，看它们是吃还是不吃？

transcript.en: JAKE: Great. Then later on, you could do a follow-up experiment adding another variable.
transcript.cn: 杰克：太好了。然后，你可以做一个后续实验，加入另一个变量。

transcript.en: Like, you could give some of the mice the chance to be more active, running on a wheel or something, and the others just sit around and don't do much.
transcript.cn: 比如，你可以给一些小白鼠活动的机会，让它们在轮子上跑什么的，而其他的小白鼠只是坐在那里，什么也不做。

transcript.en: CLARE: Or I could repeat the experiment but change the type of food I provided . . . or use mice with a different genetic structure.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：或者我可以重复这个实验，但是改变所提供的食物种类……或者使用不同基因结构的小白鼠。

transcript.en: But I think your idea would be more interesting, I might think about that some more.
transcript.cn: 但是我觉得你的想法更加有趣，我可以再考虑一下。

transcript.en: CLARE: So can I talk through a possible procedure for the experiment where mice are given a sugar supplement?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：那我能讨论一下小白鼠摄入糖分的可能的实验步骤吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: Sure. I did a similar experiment in college actually.
transcript.cn: 杰克：当然，我大学时做过一次类似的实验。

transcript.en: CLARE: Great. So how many mice would I need?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：太好了。那么我需要多少只小白鼠？

transcript.en: JAKE: I'd say about 12. And all young ones, not a mixture of old and young.
transcript.cn: 杰克：大概12只，而且都是年轻的，不能老的和年轻的混合。

transcript.en: CLARE: OK. And I'd need two groups of equal sizes, so six in each group. And how would I tell them apart?
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：好的。我需要两组小白鼠，每组6只。那我要怎么区分它们呢？

transcript.en: I suppose I could put some sort of tag on one group . . . or just mark them in some way?
transcript.cn: 我想我可以给其中一组贴上标签，或者用某种方式标记？

transcript.en: JAKE: You could use food colouring, that wouldn't hurt them.
transcript.cn: 杰克：你可以用食用色素，这不会伤害他们。

transcript.en: CLARE: Perfect. Then each group would go into a separate cage, and one group, let's call them group A, would be the control group.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：太好了。然后每组关在一个单独的笼子里 其中一组，我们叫它A组，作为对照组。

transcript.en: So they'd just have ordinary mouse food. I suppose you can buy that?
transcript.cn: 他们只吃普通的老鼠食物，你能买到吗？

transcript.en: JAKE: Yes, it comes in dry pellets.
transcript.cn: 杰克：可以，有干的颗粒。

transcript.en: CLARE: And the other group would have the same as the first group, but they'd also have the extra sugar.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：另一组的食物和第一组一样，但有额外的糖。

transcript.en: JAKE: Would you just give them straight sugar?
transcript.cn: 杰克：你会直接给它们糖吃吗？

transcript.en: CLARE: It might be better to give them something like cereal with it.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：也许给他们一些像麦片之类的东西会更好。

transcript.en: JAKE: Mmm. Then you'd need to weigh the mice, I should think once a week. And you'd need an electronic balance.
transcript.cn: 杰克：嗯。然后你得给小白鼠称体重，我想应该是一周一次。你需要一个电子天平。

transcript.en: CLARE: But we can't hold them on the balance, or it'd affect the reading.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔: 但是我们不能抓着它们放到天平上，否则会影响读数。

transcript.en: JAKE: Exactly. So you need something called a weighing chamber to stop the mice from running away.
transcript.cn: 杰克：没错，所以你需要一个叫称重室的东西，来防止小白鼠逃跑。

transcript.en: It sounds complicated, but actually you can just use a plastic box with holes in the top.
transcript.cn: 这听起来很复杂，但实际上你可以用一个顶部有洞的塑料盒子。

transcript.en: CLARE: OK. So once we've measured the weight gain of each mouse we can work out the average for each group, as well as the standard deviation.
transcript.cn: 克莱尔：好的。这样一旦我们测量了每只老鼠的体重增长，我们就可以计算出每组的平均值，以及标准差。

transcript.en: And then see where we go from there. That sounds cool, I think the students will enjoy it.
transcript.cn: 然后看看我们接下来要做什么。听起来很酷，我想学生们会喜欢的。

transcript.en: JAKE: Yes. One thing . . .
transcript.cn: 杰克：是的。还有一件事……

groups.title: Microplastics
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Where microplastics come from • fibres from some$21742$during washing • the breakdown of large pieces of plastic • waste from industry • the action of vehicle tyres on roads Effects of microplastics • They cause injuries to the$21743$of wildlife and affect their digestive systems. • They enter the food chain, e.g., in bottled and tap water, $21744$and seafood. • They may not affect human health, but they are already banned in skin cleaning products and$21745$in some countries. • Microplastics enter the soil through the air, rain and$21746$. Microplastics in the soil – a study by Anglia Ruskin University • Earthworms are important because they add$21747$to the soil. • The study aimed to find whether microplastics in earthworms affect the$21748$of plants. • The study found that microplastics caused: - $21749$loss in earthworms - fewer seeds to germinate - a rise in the level of$21750$in the soil. • The study concluded: - soil should be seen as an important natural process. - changes to soil damage both ecosystems and$21751$.

questions.analysis: 答案是“clothing”，因为题干问的是“纤维在洗涤过程中来自哪里”，而听力原文明确提到“Threads and microfibres detach from synthetic clothing every time they're put in a washing machine”，即每次将合成衣物放入洗衣机时，线和微纤维都会脱落。因此，纤维的来源是“clothing”。

答案是“mouths”，因为听力材料中明确提到“microplastics harm small creatures in a variety of ways, such as by damaging their mouths”。题干中的“injuries”对应“damaging”，“wildlife”对应“small creatures”，“digestive systems”对应“digestive system”。通过这些定位词和信号词，考生可以快速找到相关信息并确定正确答案。
答案是“salt”，因为在听力原文中提到“bottled water”和“tap water”后，紧接着说到“salt and many kinds of seafood have also been found to contain microplastics”。题干中已经提到“seafood”，因此需要填入另一个例子，即“salt”。

答案是“toothpaste”，因为听力原文明确提到在许多国家，包括英国在内，有法律禁止制造商在沐浴露、面部清洁剂和牙膏中添加塑料微珠。题干中的“banned”对应原文中的“prevents”，“skin cleaning products”对应“shower gels”和“facial cleansers”，“some countries”对应“many countries, including here in the UK”。因此，正确答案是“toothpaste”。

答案是“fertilisers/fertilizers”，因为听力原文明确提到“many of the fertilisers used by both farmers and gardeners contain microplastics”，这表明肥料是微塑料进入土壤的途径之一。题干中的定位词“air”和“rain”帮助我们快速定位到相关信息点，而答案句中的“fertilisers”直接回答了题干的问题。
答案是“nutrients”，因为听力材料中明确提到蚯蚓通过在土壤中进食，将养分混合到土壤中，从而使其更加肥沃。这与题干中的“add”相对应，说明蚯蚓将养分添加到土壤中。
正确答案是“growth”，因为题干问的是微塑料在蚯蚓体内是否会影响植物的【生长】。听力原文中明确提到研究人员的目标是发现微塑料是否会影响土壤质量，并最终抑制植物的生长（inhibit plant growth）。因此，答案是“growth”。
答案是“weight”，因为听力原文明确提到“the worms lost weight rapidly”，这与题干中的“loss in earthworms”直接对应。通过识别定位词“microplastics”、“loss”和“earthworms”，考生可以在听力过程中迅速定位到相关信息，并准确填入答案。
这道题的正确答案是“acid”，因为听力材料明确提到“there was an increase in the amount of acid found in the soil”，这与题干中的“a rise in the level of【    】in the soil”直接对应。通过识别和理解定位词和信号词，考生可以准确找到答案所在的句子，并填入正确的单词。
答案是“society”，因为在听力材料中明确提到“not only ecosystems but also the whole of society are negatively impacted”，这表明土壤的变化不仅损害生态系统，还损害社会。题干要求填入另一个受影响的对象，因此答案是“society”。

transcript.en: PART 4
In today's lecture, I'm going to be talking about microplastics.
transcript.cn: 在今天的讲座中，我将谈谈微塑料。

transcript.en: Microplastics are tiny pieces of plastic smaller than five millimetres in size.
transcript.cn: 微塑料是指小于五毫米的塑料碎片。

transcript.en: Recently there's been a greater awareness that there are large quantities of plastic waste - big and small - in the environment.
transcript.cn: 最近，人们越来越意识到，环境中存在着大量大大小小的塑料垃圾。

transcript.en: The amount of plastic waste in the oceans has received widespread attention, but far less is known about the effects of microplastics in freshwater and particularly in soil.
transcript.cn: 海洋中的塑料垃圾数量已引起广泛关注，但人们对淡水中，尤其是土壤中的微塑料的影响却知之甚少。

transcript.en: Microplastics can enter the environment via a number of different sources.
transcript.cn: 微塑料可通过多种不同途径进入环境。

transcript.en: Threads and microfibres detach from synthetic clothing every time they're put in a washing machine, and these find their way into the water system.
transcript.cn: 合成纤维衣物每次放进洗衣机后都会脱落线头和微纤维，这些都会进入水系统。

transcript.en: Other sources include big pieces of plastic waste that are already in the environment, and these break down into microscopic particles over a period of time.
transcript.cn: 其他途径包括已经存在于环境中的大块塑料废弃物，这些废弃物在一段时间后会分解成微小颗粒。

transcript.en: On a larger scale, factory waste is another route, as are tyres which wear down as cars, lorries and so on travel along road surfaces.
transcript.cn: 从更大的范围看，工厂废料是另外一种途径，汽车、卡车等在路面行驶时磨损的轮胎也是如此。

transcript.en: We already understand some of the impacts of microplastics from studies involving fish and other animals.
transcript.cn: 通过对鱼类和其他动物的研究，我们已经了解了微塑料的一些影响。

transcript.en: There is evidence that microplastics harm small creatures in a variety of ways, such as by damaging their mouths, or by impairing their ability to feed, for example when microplastics get lodged in their digestive system.
transcript.cn: 有证据表明，微塑料会以多种方式对小型生物造成伤害，例如损坏它们的口腔，或损害它们的进食能力，例如当微塑料卡在它们的消化系统中时。

transcript.en: Surprisingly perhaps, it is likely that humans consume microplastics, as these have been detected in a wide range of food and drink products, including bottled water, as well as in water that comes direct from the tap.
transcript.cn: 令人惊讶的是，人类很可能也会食用微塑料，因为在包括瓶装水在内的各种食品和饮料中以及在自来水中，都检测到了微塑料。

transcript.en: What's more, salt and many kinds of seafood have also been found to contain microplastics.
transcript.cn: 此外，盐和多种海产品也被发现含有微塑料。

transcript.en: However, it's important to underline that there is not yet conclusive proof that microplastics cause significant harm to people.
transcript.cn: 不过，需要强调的是，目前还没有确凿证据证明微塑料会对人体造成重大伤害。

transcript.en: In many countries, including here in the UK, there is legislation which prevents manufacturers from adding plastic microbeads to shower gels, facial cleansers and toothpaste.
transcript.cn: 包括英国在内的许多国家都立法禁止制造商在沐浴露、洁面乳和牙膏中添加塑料微珠。

transcript.en: It is very difficult to accurately estimate the total amount of microplastic particles in the soil as they can be hard to detect, but we do know they are carried in the air and deposited in the soil by rain.
transcript.cn: 要准确估计土壤中的微塑料颗粒总量非常困难，因为它们很难被检测到，但我们确实知道它们随空气传播，并通过降雨沉积在土壤中。

transcript.en: What's more, many of the fertilisers used by both farmers and gardeners contain microplastics.
transcript.cn: 此外，农民和园丁使用的许多肥料中都含有微塑料。

transcript.en: A team from the Anglia Ruskin University in Cambridge has carried out a study of the effects of microplastics on the digestive tracts of earthworms.
transcript.cn: 剑桥安格利亚鲁斯金大学的一个研究小组进行了一项关于微塑料对蚯蚓消化道影响的研究。

transcript.en: These worms, which live in topsoil, are an essential component of our agricultural system.
transcript.cn: 这些生活在表土中的蠕虫是我们农业系统的重要组成部分。

transcript.en: By feeding on soil, they mix nutrients into it, thereby making it more fertile.
transcript.cn: 它们以土壤为食，将养分混合到土壤中，从而使土壤更加肥沃。

transcript.en: The researchers set out to discover whether the introduction of microplastics into the soil - and the subsequent ingestion of these by earthworms - would impact soil quality and ultimately inhibit plant growth. The short answer was, yes, it did.
transcript.cn: 研究人员试图研究，在土壤中引入微塑料——以及蚯蚓随后摄入这些微塑料——是否会影响土壤质量，并最终抑制植物生长。简而言之：会。

transcript.en: After placing three different types of microplastic particles into the soil, they planted perennial rye grass.
transcript.cn: 将三种不同类型的微塑料颗粒放入土壤后，他们种植了多年生黑麦草。

transcript.en: The particles of microplastic, which included biodegradable PLA and conventional high-density polyethylene, or HDPE, were then ingested by the earthworms in the soil.
transcript.cn: 这些微塑料颗粒包括可生物降解的聚乳酸（PLA）和传统的高密度聚乙烯（HDPE），随后被土壤中的蚯蚓摄入。

transcript.en: The result was that the worms lost weight rapidly.
transcript.cn: 结果，蚯蚓的体重迅速下降。

transcript.en: What's more, a lower percentage than normal of the rye grass seeds germinated, and the researchers concluded that this was a direct result of the earthworms being unable to fulfil their normal role in making soil more fertile.
transcript.cn: 此外，黑麦草种子的发芽率也低于正常水平，研究人员得出结论，这是蚯蚓无法发挥其正常作用，使土壤更加肥沃的直接结果。

transcript.en: The team also discovered that there was an increase in the amount of acid found in the soil, and this was attributed mainly to the microplastic particles from conventional HDPE plastic.
transcript.cn: 研究小组还发现，土壤中的酸含量有所增加，这主要归因于传统高密度聚乙烯（HDPE）塑料中的微塑料颗粒。

transcript.en: The conclusions of the study make for very interesting reading - I've included the reference in the notes to give you at the end of this session.
transcript.cn: 这项研究的结论读起来非常有趣——我在本节课的课堂笔记中附上了这些参考文献。

transcript.en: To summarise, the authors proposed the idea that we need to regard soil as we would regard any other process in nature.
transcript.cn: 总而言之，作者提出的观点是，我们需要像看待自然界中的任何其他过程一样看待土壤。

transcript.en: This means we should accept the implications of soil being dependent on decaying and dead matter constantly being passed through the bodies of earthworms.
transcript.cn: 这意味着我们应该接受土壤依赖于蚯蚓体内不断传递的腐朽物质所带来的影响。

transcript.en: That is, when soil becomes impoverished by the presence of microplastics, not only ecosystems but also the whole of society are negatively impacted.
transcript.cn: 也就是说，当土壤因为微塑料的存在而变得贫瘠时，不仅生态系统，整个社会都会受到负面影响。
